title: salix × pedionoma
intro: Salix × pedionoma, also known as the weeping willow or white willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Salicaceae family. Its bark and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The health benefits of Salix × pedionoma include reducing pain and fever, as well as acting as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
